method: Size-matched vocabulary-restriction test (TTR + normalized entropy) of entry-words (word tokens whose next stream token is 'num') vs non-entry words. Larger group subsampled to smaller group size; >=500 bootstrap resamples for matched metrics; label-permutation p (shuffle entry/non labels among pooled words, recompute matched TTR gap). Positive control: (a) DETECT planted restricted+skewed entry vocab vs diverse non-entry vocab; (b) FALSE-POSITIVE same-vocab different-size splits. Cross-site per site with >=20 in each group; leave-one-site-out on Haghia Triada.
result: Entry-words are NOT globally more restricted than non-entry words under the size-matched test. Global matched TTR gap (non-entry minus entry) = +0.043 (entry slightly lower TTR) BUT normalized entropy gap = -0.058 (entry HIGHER entropy, i.e. MORE even, opposite of restriction); direction = MIXED. Label-permutation one-sided p for 'entry more restricted' = 0.97 (entry-words are not significantly more concentrated; the opposite tail is p=0.03). The dominant site Haghia Triada (66% of entry-words) shows entry-words LESS restricted (gap_ttr=-0.062, p=1.0). Four smaller sites (Khania, Phaistos, Zakros, Arkhalkhori) show a TTR-only entry-more-restricted signal (p<=0.05) but with near-zero entropy gaps. Leave-one-site-out (drop HT) yields p=0.002 entry-more-restricted, but this is an artifact of removing the dominant counter-evidence site and the entropy gap remains negligible (0.013). PC PASSED (detect p=0.003 correct direction; false-positive rate 0.0 over 25 same-vocab splits). Because the GLOBAL test is not significant for entry-more-restricted (p=0.97) and the entropy metric runs opposite, there is no robust commodity-lexicon restriction.
key_findings: GLOBAL: entry-words are NOT significantly more restricted than non-entry words under the size-matched test (label-permutation one-sided p=0.97 for entry-more-restricted; the opposite tail p=0.03). The verdict gate on global significance FAILS.

The two restriction metrics DISAGREE globally: entry-words have slightly LOWER matched TTR (raw 0.462 vs 0.388; size-matched bootstrap-mean 0.329 vs 0.372) but HIGHER normalized entropy (0.903 vs 0.845) -> direction MIXED. A true commodity lexicon requires BOTH lower; entropy runs opposite, so entry-words are if anything MORE evenly/uniformly distributed, not concentrated.
The dominant site Haghia Triada (686 entry-words, 66% of the global entry count) shows entry-words LESS restricted (gap_ttr=-0.062, p=1.0), which drives the global null.

Four smaller sites (Khania, Phaistos, Zakros, Arkhalkhori) show a TTR-only entry-more-restricted signal (p<=0.05), but in every case the normalized-entropy gap is near-zero or slightly negative -- the effect is a TTR artifact, not a genuine concentration.

Leave-one-site-out (drop HT) flips to p=0.002 entry-more-restricted, but this reflects removal of the dominant counter-evidence site rather than a robust cross-site lexicon, and the LOO entropy gap remains negligible (0.013).

POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED: the machinery detects a planted restricted+skewed vocabulary (detect p=0.003, correct direction) and has a 0.0 false-positive rate over 25 same-vocabulary different-size splits -- confirming the size-matching removes the TTR sample-size confound. The null result is therefore trustworthy, not a machinery failure.
successor_hypotheses: E054: Positional vocabulary restriction -- test whether words in SPECIFIC stream positions (e.g. line-initial, post-divider) rather than numeral-adjacency show concentration; the HT counter-result suggests numeral-adjacency is not the right locus.

E055: Quantity-conditioned lexicon -- restrict entry-words to those followed by LARGE numerals (top-quartile values) vs small numerals; a commodity lexicon may appear only for high-volume counted items.
E056: Sign-level (not word-level) restriction -- repeat the TTR/entropy test on individual SIGNS within entry-words vs non-entry words; restriction may live at the sub-word level.
E057: HT-internal structure -- investigate why Haghia Triada entry-words are LESS restricted (larger administrative vocabulary?); stratify HT by support/context (tablet vs other) and re-test.

E058: Repetition-rate rather than type diversity -- test whether entry-words have higher per-form token counts (a few forms counted very often) even when entropy is high, using a repetition/concentration index (e.g. Herfindahl) instead of normalized entropy.

E059: Cross-site TTR-only effect follow-up -- the 4 smaller sites show a consistent TTR gap; test whether this survives a stricter multiple-comparison correction and larger per-site samples before claiming a site-local lexicon.
non_circular: Forms anonymous (sign tuples only, never decoded). Entry defined by OBSERVED numeral-adjacency in the stream, not by meaning. Pure distributional vocabulary structure (TTR + normalized entropy). No phonetics, no semantics, no numeral values used. LB control is synthetic only because Linear B lacks the stream numeral-adjacency structure required by this test -- stated explicitly. Positive control uses synthetic planted vocabularies. Size-matching removes the TTR sample-size confound (verified by 0.0 false-positive rate on same-vocab different-size splits).
deviations: Permutation bootstrap counts reduced (global n_perm=500-1000 with n_boot=100-300 inside permutation; cross-site n_perm=300 n_boot=300; LOO n_perm=500 n_boot=300) to fit synchronous runtime -- gap statistic is stable at n_boot>=100 (gap_ttr=0.0425 at n_boot=100 vs 0.0430 at n_boot=500).

pc_detect planted entry vocabulary made SKEWED (Zipf weights) rather than uniform, because a genuine 'commodity lexicon' is small AND unevenly used; a uniform small set has high normalized entropy and would not represent the hypothesis. This is a PC-design refinement consistent with the prereg's 'small form set' intent; self-check uses the same skewed design.
